policy: Install the curated core by default. Treat the wider research zoo as target-fit adapters.

projects.name: AFL
projects.why: Original baseline; AFL++ supersedes it for this pipeline.

projects.name: AFL++
projects.why: Primary campaign engine: LTO, CMPLOG, persistent mode, custom mutators, QEMU/FRIDA support.

projects.name: libFuzzer
projects.why: Best smoke, repro, and minimization path for LLVM harnesses.

projects.name: Honggfuzz
projects.fit: source C/C++ and some binary workflows
projects.why: Secondary coverage-guided engine when target shape supports it.

projects.name: Radamsa
projects.why: Useful seed mutator; not always packaged on Ubuntu.

projects.name: WinAFL
projects.why: Not useful on this Linux VPS unless Windows target workflow is added.

projects.name: afl-dyninst
projects.why: Binary instrumentation adapter; prefer AFL++ QEMU/FRIDA first.

projects.name: TriforceAFL
projects.why: Kernel/full-system fuzzing is out of v1 scope.

projects.name: AFL-abiondo
projects.why: Historical QEMU improvement; AFL++ carries modern binary modes.

projects.name: aflsmart
projects.why: Grammar-aware ideas map to dictionaries/custom mutators.

projects.name: AFLFast
projects.why: Power schedule ideas are already in AFL++.

projects.name: WineAFLplusplusDEMO
projects.why: Demo/helper workflow, not a Linux C/C++ default.

projects.name: afl-sensitive
projects.why: Use as coverage-guidance concept, not default tool.

projects.name: Redqueen
projects.why: CMPLOG/Redqueen-style solving is integrated in AFL++.

projects.name: afl-pin
projects.why: Specialized binary adapter.

projects.name: Driller
projects.why: Heavy symbolic execution, only for stuck campaigns.

projects.name: Angora
projects.why: Research engine; use ideas for hard-branch escalation.

projects.name: VUzzer
projects.why: Target-specific binary workflow.

projects.name: Manul
projects.why: Not core while AFL++/libFuzzer cover current scope.

projects.name: QSYM
projects.why: Escalation when CMPLOG/dictionaries cannot cross checks.

projects.name: netafl
projects.why: Out of Linux source C/C++ v1 scope.

projects.name: Unicorefuzz
projects.why: Kernel fuzzing adapter.

projects.name: SharpFuzz
projects.why: Unsupported language/runtime for this pipeline.

projects.name: Nautilus
projects.why: Use when a grammar exists and byte mutation stalls.

projects.name: frida-js-afl-instr
projects.why: Binary/runtime adapter.

projects.name: UnTracer-AFL
projects.why: Useful performance idea, not default deployment.

projects.name: kleefl
projects.why: Escalation for seed generation.

projects.name: AFLGo
projects.why: Use for patch-diff/CVE/line-target campaigns.

projects.name: afl-dynamorio
projects.why: Specialized binary adapter.

projects.name: FairFuzz
projects.why: Rare/explore schedules are represented in AFL++ campaigns.

projects.name: Superion
projects.why: Use only for structured parsers with AST support.

projects.name: NEUZZ
projects.why: Research prototype; not operational core.

projects.name: FuzzFactory
projects.why: Concept maps to future custom scoring.

projects.name: kAFL
projects.why: Requires hypervisor workflow.

projects.name: AFLNet
projects.why: Add only for stateful network targets.

projects.name: Grimoire
projects.why: Structured-language escalation.

projects.name: JQF
projects.why: Unsupported runtime for v1.

projects.name: PerfFuzz
projects.why: Useful for DoS/performance campaigns, not memory-corruption core.

projects.name: Ankou
projects.why: Coverage-guidance concept.

projects.name: SAVIOR
projects.why: Patch/bug-driven escalation.

projects.name: T-Fuzz
projects.why: May over-approximate; never report transformed-target crashes without original repro.

projects.name: afl-compiler-fuzzer
projects.why: Target-specific for language/compiler parsers.

projects.name: Eclipser
projects.why: Binary-only escalation.

projects.name: ParmeSan
projects.why: Important for bug-directed coverage heuristics.

projects.name: Weizz
projects.why: Use concept for automatic structure inference.

projects.name: MOpt-AFL
projects.why: MOpt-style scheduling is available in AFL++.

projects.name: Kirenenko
projects.why: Specialized path-solving engine.

projects.name: UAFuzz
projects.why: Use when analysis points to UAF operation sequences.

projects.name: Intriguer
projects.why: Concept maps to structured-field guidance.

projects.name: TinyAFL
projects.why: Windows/binary adapter.

projects.name: DeepFuzzer
projects.why: Concept only.

projects.name: MEUZZ
projects.why: Concept only.

projects.name: winafl-powermopt
projects.why: Windows adapter.

projects.name: SymQEMU
projects.why: Heavy binary-only escalation.

projects.name: uAFL
projects.why: Use as UAF campaign concept.

projects.name: Fuzzilli
projects.why: Only for JS engine targets.

projects.name: WINNIE
projects.why: Windows closed-source workflow.

projects.name: fuzzolic
projects.why: Approximate solving concept.

projects.name: aflteam
projects.why: Use concepts for multi-worker campaign allocation.

projects.name: Mu2
projects.why: Coverage guidance concept.

projects.name: datAFLow
projects.why: Future coverage signal.

projects.name: sFuzz
projects.why: Architecture-specific binary adapter.

projects.name: LibAFL
projects.why: Framework for building fuzzers; we are not building a new fuzzer in v1.

projects.name: StateAFL
projects.why: Network/state adapter.

projects.name: K-Scheduler
projects.why: Use as guidance idea for prioritizing seeds.

projects.name: binary-safe-c-fuzzer
projects.why: Adopt explicit byte lengths, non-blocking/timeout thinking, signal classification, artifact caps.

projects.name: C-Mutator
projects.why: Adopt signal classification; do not replace AFL++/libFuzzer.

projects.name: AutoPatch-LLM
projects.why: After confirmed crash, generate candidate fixes and retest.

projects.name: NeonOS-Simulator
projects.why: Use as example of project-native fuzz toggles and C API harnessability.
